questions: You mentioned getting stressed about the MCP spec finalizing while you were building — what made you decide to learn it as it landed rather than wait for stability, and did that choice actually pay off?

Walk me through why you put the risk classification tool on MCP instead of Skills, when so much of the other judgment work landed in Skills — what does 'three different callers must all agree' actually mean in practice?

You seeded two deliberately broken claims to 'give next week's adversary something concrete to attack' — was that the main reason, or are you also testing that your own evaluation catches them before the adversarial agent even gets a turn?
